Inside, the Swift Spin 14 AI can be configured with either a Qualcomm Snapdragon X2 Plus or Snap

item.title: Pam Bondi recovering after thyroid cancer surgery while remaining active in Trump administration advisory roles
item.description:
Former U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi is recovering after undergoing surgery for thyroid cancer a few weeks ago, according to comments she made in an interview with CNN. Bondi stated that she is still receiving follow-up treatment but emphasized that she is “doing well” as she continues her recovery process. Her diagnosis and treatment come shortly after she left her position at the Department of Justice earlier in 2026.
Bondi’s departure from the Justice Department followed political tensions during her tenure. President Donald Trump removed her from the role in April, criticizing her for not pursuing legal actions against individuals he viewed as political adversaries. Her time in office also drew bipartisan scrutiny over her handling of documents related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, an issue that contributed to public and congressional attention on her leadership.
Despite stepping down from her cabinet position, Bondi remains involved in government-related activitie

item.title: Several Musicians Withdraw from Freedom 250 Festival Over Political Concerns
item.description: Several high-profile artists have withdrawn from the Freedom 250 festival, an event linked to former President Donald Trump, amid concerns over its political affiliations. The festival, which is being held on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., is part of the country's 250th-anniversary celebrations and features a lineup spanning multiple music genres. Morris Day, the famed Minneapolis performer, publicly denied involvement despite being listed in promotional materials. Similarly, Young MC, known for 'Bust a Move,' confirmed he would not participate, citing a lack of transparency about the event's political ties. Other advertised performers included Milli Vanilli, Vanilla Ice, and Flo Rida, though it remains unclear which acts were formally booked. The festival organizers have claimed the event is non-partisan, but media coverage, including SPIN magazine, describes it as 'Trump-backed.' Artists have expressed discomfort with being associated with a politically charged event, emphasi

item.title: Spencer Pratt claims divine support in Los Angeles mayoral race during Fox News interview
item.description:
Spencer Pratt, a former reality TV personality and Republican candidate for mayor of Los Angeles, appeared on Fox & Friends to discuss his campaign ahead of the June 2 primary election. During the interview, he suggested that he believes divine intervention is guiding his candidacy, stating that he feels “God wants me to just win with 51%” and end the race outright in the primary. Pratt entered the mayoral contest in January following the loss of his Pacific Palisades home in the 2025 wildfires, and has since positioned himself as an outsider candidate despite his Republican affiliation in a heavily Democratic city.
The article highlights Pratt’s unexpectedly strong fundraising performance, reporting that his campaign raised approximately $2.72 million in a recent filing period, significantly outpacing incumbent Mayor Karen Bass and fellow progressive challenger Nithya Raman. A recent poll showed Bass leading the race with Pratt in second place and Raman close behind, suggesting a com

item.title: Republican Officials Cautious About Attending Bruce Springsteen Concerts Amid Trump Tensions
item.description: Several Republican operatives and Trump administration staffers who are fans of Bruce Springsteen face a difficult choice as the rock star tours with his 'No Kings Tour.' They fear that attending his concerts could lead to negative consequences from former President Donald Trump. Sources report that GOP staffers are hesitant to publicly show support for Springsteen, fearing professional repercussions. Doug Heye, a GOP consultant and longtime Springsteen fan, noted the possibility that employees could be spotted at shows and subsequently pressured at work. Springsteen's tour, officially the 'Land of Hope and Dreams American Tour,' features politically charged performances, opening and closing with protest anthems. Meanwhile, Trump has escalated his feud with Springsteen, criticizing the musician publicly. Even prominent Republican figures from New Jersey, including former Governor Chris Christie, have not commented on their concert attendance. The climate has led some Republican fans to

item.title: JPMorgan Chase open to major acquisitions of up to $20 billion under Jamie Dimon
item.description:
JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon indicated that the bank could pursue significant acquisitions over the next few years, potentially deploying between $10 billion and $20 billion to purchase a suitable target. Speaking at a financial conference in New York, Dimon emphasized that the bank remains open to opportunities but is not aggressively pursuing mergers and acquisitions as a core growth strategy. Instead, he framed M&A activity as a secondary option, used only when it clearly strengthens the company’s existing operations and long-term strategy.
Dimon stressed that any potential acquisition must meet strict criteria, including seamless integration into JPMorgan’s operations, alignment with its corporate culture, and a direct contribution to strengthening core business lines. He cautioned against relying on acquisitions to compensate for weak organic growth, arguing that companies often misuse M&A as a substitute for improving sales, innovation, and operational efficiency.
The CEO als

item.title: Trump refiles defamation lawsuit against Wall Street Journal citing Maxwell interview
item.description: President Donald Trump has refiled his $10 billion defamation lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal in federal court in Miami. The lawsuit targets a 2025 Wall Street Journal article claiming a lewd birthday letter, allegedly authored by Trump, was included in a 2003 album Ghislaine Maxwell compiled for Jeffrey Epstein's 50th birthday. Trump's amended complaint references a July 2025 interview with Maxwell conducted by Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, who was then Deputy Attorney General. The interview, conducted under limited immunity, included Maxwell stating she did not remember Trump submitting any letter or note. Trump's legal team argues that Maxwell's lack of recollection undermines the Journal's reporting and that the newspaper published without producing the letter itself. The original lawsuit was dismissed in April by U.S. District Judge Darrin Gayles, citing failure to meet the actual malice standard required in defamation cases involving public figures. Dow Jones, the

item.title: Pentagon Loan to Vulcan Elements Accelerated by White House Intervention Linked to Trump Jr.
item.description: Defense Department records and reporting by ProPublica reveal that a $620 million Pentagon loan to the North Carolina startup Vulcan Elements, linked to Donald Trump Jr., was directly influenced by White House intervention. Peter Navarro, a senior advisor and friend of Trump Jr., initiated the request, which led to an unusually rapid review and approval process compared to other companies. The loan significantly increased Vulcan’s valuation, benefiting Trump Jr.'s venture firm, 1789 Capital, which had invested in the company prior to the announcement. While White House and Pentagon officials claimed that Trump Jr. had no involvement, the timeline and personal connections raise ethical questions. The Office of Strategic Capital, responsible for the loan, expanded its funding authority under the Trump administration, prioritizing speed and direct recruitment of companies over the prior methodical application process. Vulcan, a young firm focused on rare-earth magnets critical for militar

item.title: General Compute bets on inference-focused AI infrastructure using SambaNova chips
item.description:
The article explores how the surging demand for AI compute, especially for inference workloads, is reshaping the infrastructure landscape and creating opportunities for new players. A startup called General Compute is positioning itself as an “inference neocloud,” focusing on providing optimized compute for AI models during their deployment phase rather than training. The company recently raised a $15 million seed round at a $60 million post-money valuation, led by FUSE VC with participation from Carya Venture Partners and Village Global Ventures.
Instead of relying primarily on GPUs, General Compute is turning to specialized inference chips developed by SambaNova, an Intel-backed chipmaker. These chips are designed to improve performance during inference by using higher memory capacity and more efficient architectures for handling context-heavy workloads. The company claims these chips can deliver between 600 and 700 tokens per second, compared to roughly 250 tokens per second on tra

item.title: Robert Reich argues Trump’s recent behavior and health concerns raise questions about his fitness for office
item.description:
In this opinion article, former U.S. Labor Secretary and public policy professor Robert Reich argues that President Donald Trump’s recent public behavior and repeated medical visits have intensified concerns about his physical and mental fitness for office. Reich, who notes that he is nearly the same age as Trump, reflects on the realities of aging and suggests that cognitive and physical decline can affect anyone approaching 80 years old, especially someone serving in a demanding role such as the presidency.
The article discusses Trump’s recent visit to Walter Reed National Military Medical Center, officially described by the White House as a routine medical and dental evaluation. Reich questions the transparency of Trump’s health disclosures, citing conflicting explanations about medical scans and visible physical issues such as bruised hands, swollen ankles, fatigue, and unusual behavior during public appearances.
Reich also references several controversial statements and social m

item.title: Kubb Fanless Mini PC Updated with Intel Panther Lake CPU and Premium Pricing
item.description: French manufacturer Bleu Jour has updated its Kubb Fanless mini PC with Intel's latest Panther Lake CPU, the Core Ultra X7 358H, paired with an Arc B390 integrated GPU. The fanless design remains, utilizing aluminum chassis fins for passive cooling in a compact 12 × 12 cm footprint, weighing 2.3 kg. The base configuration includes Windows 11 Pro, 16 GB of DDR5-5600 RAM in dual channel, and a 1 TB M.2 NVMe Gen4 SSD, priced at €1,850, which is €355 higher than the previous Core Ultra 5 225H model. Optional upgrades allow up to 64 GB DDR5-6400 RAM for €545.90 extra and a 4 TB SSD for an additional €526, bringing the fully maxed-out configuration to €2,921.90. The mini PC supports a wide range of ports, including Thunderbolt 4, USB 3.2, HDMI 2.1, and dual RJ45. While the high price places it near AMD Ryzen AI-powered mini PCs, its passive cooling and compact form factor appeal to niche users seeking silent operation and small desktop footprints. Enthusiasts may consider DIY fanless builds

item.title: Study identifies GPNMB protein as a driver of Parkinson’s disease progression and a potential therapeutic target
item.description:
Researchers from the Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania have identified a brain immune protein, GPNMB, as a key factor in the progression of Parkinson’s disease. The study suggests that this protein helps facilitate the spread of toxic alpha-synuclein aggregates between neurons, accelerating neurodegeneration over time. In Parkinson’s disease, alpha-synuclein forms harmful clumps inside neurons, which then propagate to neighboring cells, worsening symptoms such as tremors, balance issues, and movement difficulties.
The research team discovered that microglia, the brain’s immune cells, release increased amounts of GPNMB in response to neuronal damage. This protein is then processed and secreted, where it appears to enhance the uptake of alpha-synuclein by nearby healthy neurons, effectively amplifying disease spread. In laboratory experiments using cultured neurons, the researchers developed monoclonal antibodies designed to block GPNMB. These antibodies succ

item.title: Astrophysical Calibration Improves Accuracy of Gravitational Wave Measurements
item.description: Since the first confirmed detection of gravitational waves in 2017, over 390 events have been recorded thanks to the combined efforts of LIGO, Virgo, and KAGRA detectors. The sensitivity of these detectors is crucial but can vary, sometimes resulting in data that requires correction. Researchers have developed a new method called Astrophysical Calibration, which can retroactively adjust detector data, similar to how Auto-Tune corrects pitch in music. This technique was applied to two recent signals, GW240925 and GW250207, which were observed during the LVK Collaboration's fourth observing run. In both cases, calibration errors or missing on-site measurements made accurate data interpretation challenging. By comparing observed signals with theoretical predictions from Einstein’s General Relativity and cross-checking with other detectors, the team could correct the distortions and precisely determine the properties of the black hole mergers. GW240925 involved black holes of 9 and 7 solar

item.title: Stephanie O’Connor Explores Pregnancy and Microchimerism Through Composite Photography in The Waves Came in Like Horses
item.description:
The LensCulture interview “The Waves Came in Like Horses” presents the photographic work of Stephanie O’Connor, a series that blends layered imagery to explore themes of pregnancy, identity, and bodily transformation. Originally started during the COVID-19 lockdown, the project initially consisted of experimental composites made from old photographs, found negatives, and fragmented visual materials. At that stage, O’Connor set the work aside, feeling it lacked conceptual depth.
The project gained new meaning years later when the artist became pregnant. During this period, she re-engaged with the earlier fragments, now experiencing them through the lens of bodily change and internal movement. The sensations of pregnancy—described as shifting, kinetic, and at times unfamiliar—transformed how she approached image-making, infusing the work with emotional and physiological resonance.
Central to the project is the scientific concept of microchimerism, the phenomenon in which cells are exch

item.title: Hotel reservation data exploited in targeted phishing scams affecting travelers worldwide
item.description:
A new security investigation has revealed that customer booking data from more than 350 hotels, vacation rentals, motels, and guesthouses across 50 countries may have been accessed and weaponized in sophisticated phishing campaigns. Cybercriminals are reportedly using real reservation details—such as guest names, check-in dates, and hotel information—to craft highly convincing scam messages sent via SMS, email, and messaging apps like WhatsApp.
According to researchers at Norton, these attacks rely on “reservation hijacking,” where stolen or obtained booking information is inserted into fake verification or payment requests. The use of authentic travel details makes the phishing attempts significantly more believable, increasing the likelihood that victims will click malicious links and provide sensitive financial information. In some cases, the fake websites even include chatbots designed to instantly collect and transmit user-entered data to attackers.
The analysis suggests that ho

item.title: Retired Florida judge and over 120 legal experts file ethics complaint against former Attorney General Pam Bondi
item.description: A coalition of more than 120 Florida judges, law professors, and attorneys, led by retired Florida Supreme Court Chief Justice Peggy Quince, has filed a formal ethics complaint against former U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi with the Florida Bar. The complaint comes after President Donald Trump dismissed Bondi in April 2026, removing the previous barrier that prevented the Bar from investigating sitting federal officials. The filing accuses Bondi of pressuring Department of Justice attorneys to violate ethical standards under threat of termination and mishandling the release of Jeffrey Epstein-related documents. Critics claim the January document release exposed unredacted victim information, including names, birth dates, and photos, described by some attorneys as one of the worst violations of victim privacy in U.S. history. Additional allegations include authorizing prosecutions without probable cause against political adversaries such as former FBI Director James Comey and New York A

item.title: US funding cuts leave Ebola research network unable to support outbreak response in Africa
item.description:
A growing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo’s Ituri province is unfolding without the support of a key international research network, after its funding was cut by the Trump administration. The Centers for Research in Emerging Infectious Diseases (CREID), created by the US National Institutes of Health in 2020 during the COVID-19 pandemic, was designed to study viruses that spill over from animals to humans, including Ebola-related pathogens, across multiple high-risk regions in Africa and other parts of the world.
The network consisted of 10 global research sites and received roughly $82 million in NIH funding over five years. Its work included disease surveillance, genomic sequencing, and development of diagnostic tools for emerging infectious diseases. However, in June of last year, CREID centers were issued a stop-work order, with federal officials citing concerns that the research was “unsafe for Americans” and not a good use of taxpayer money. The decision was i

item.title: CNN panel debates criticism of White House UFC event tied to Trump’s birthday celebration
item.description:
A CNN discussion featured a heated exchange over criticism surrounding plans for a UFC event being staged at the White House South Lawn. The event, reportedly tied to the nation’s 250th anniversary and President Donald Trump’s upcoming 80th birthday, includes the construction of an octagon-shaped cage for a mixed martial arts bout. Concerns raised by commentators focused on both the symbolism and the appropriateness of hosting a professional fighting event at the presidential residence, as well as the blending of political branding with entertainment and sports.
Mixed martial arts analyst Luke Thomas argued that some critiques of the setup risked being overly judgmental or “elitist,” though he emphasized his main concern was the political relationship between the UFC and Trump’s administration rather than the event itself. He suggested the controversy reflects deeper issues about political influence and spectacle rather than simply the aesthetics or tone of the celebration.
Former Wh

item.title: Bruce Springsteen announces political music festival and protest-themed performance near Washington, D.C. ahead of Election Day
item.description:
Bruce Springsteen used a recent concert at Nationals Park in Washington, D.C. to deliver a strongly political message and announce plans for a large-scale music and activism event scheduled for October. During his May 27 performance with the E Street Band, Springsteen criticized Donald Trump and voiced opposition to the administration’s immigration policies, including leading chants calling for opposition to ICE enforcement.
At the concert, Springsteen revealed plans for a festival titled “Power to the People,” which is set to take place on October 3 at Merriweather Post Pavilion in Columbia, Maryland, near Washington, D.C. The event is described as a “night of music and resistance,” organized in collaboration with Rage Against The Machine guitarist Tom Morello, who is overseeing the festival. Springsteen announced that he would return to the stage for the event and perform alongside Morello, with additional appearances expected.
The festival lineup is reported to include major arti

item.title: Goldman and Lander clash over Israel policy in competitive NYC congressional primary
item.description:
A heated debate over Israel has become a central dividing line in the Democratic primary between U.S. Rep. Dan Goldman and former New York City Comptroller Brad Lander in a closely watched Manhattan-Brooklyn congressional district. Both candidates are Jewish Democrats, but they differ in tone and emphasis on Israel-related policy, particularly amid rising progressive criticism of U.S. support for the Israeli government. Goldman describes himself as a “progressive Zionist,” emphasizing Israel’s right to exist as a Jewish state and advocating for a two-state solution. He has defended his voting record supporting U.S. military aid to Israel and pushed back against what he calls overly broad criticism of pro-Israel advocacy groups such as AIPAC, though he has also said he returned contributions linked to the organization.
Lander, meanwhile, has positioned himself as more critical of Israeli government policy, arguing that Israel’s actions in Gaza and the West Bank are incompatible with de

item.title: Matthias Forster’s Photographic Exploration of Human Control and Chance in Everyday Landscapes
item.description:
This LensCulture feature presents the work of Swiss photographer Matthias Forster and his series titled “Homo Faber and Divine Providence,” which explores the relationship between human control and the unpredictable forces that shape everyday environments. Through his camera, Forster observes ordinary landscapes and built environments, revealing unexpected moments of tension, humor, and visual contradiction.
The article frames his work around the philosophical idea of “homo faber,” meaning “man the maker,” which emphasizes humanity’s ability to shape the world through tools, labor, and creativity. This concept is contrasted with “divine providence,” representing forces outside human control—whether interpreted as fate, nature, or spirituality. Forster’s photography sits at the intersection of these two ideas, showing how human ambition and planning often collide with randomness and unintended outcomes.
His images capture scenes such as cell towers, nuclear reactors, suburban gardens,

item.title: Key developments on May 28: DOJ probe into E. Jean Carroll, renewed Iran–US strikes, Trump health update, redistricting shifts, and Laos cave rescue effort
item.description:
This briefing outlines five major news developments for May 28 covering legal, geopolitical, political, and humanitarian stories. First, the U.S. Department of Justice has opened a criminal investigation into E. Jean Carroll, who previously accused President Donald Trump of sexual assault. The probe is focused on whether Carroll may have committed perjury in testimony tied to her civil lawsuits against Trump, in which juries awarded her millions in damages. Trump is currently appealing those rulings.
Second, tensions between Iran and the United States have escalated again as both sides exchanged new strikes, threatening a fragile ceasefire. These developments come amid ongoing and fluid negotiations, with President Trump expressing optimism about reaching a favorable agreement to end the conflict. The renewed military activity raises concerns about broader regional instability.
Third, President Trump’s health was again in the spotlight after his annual physical exam. He reported that

item.title: White House Staff Directed to Focus on Messaging as Trump Pursues Personal Projects Amid Falling Approval
item.description: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles has reportedly instructed aides to follow a 'let Trump be Trump' approach, allowing President Donald Trump to focus on personal projects rather than addressing pressing national issues. According to MS NOW, while Americans are frustrated with rising costs of living and the unpopular Iran war, Trump has prioritized initiatives such as constructing a new White House ballroom, renovating the National Mall reflecting pool, and establishing a $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund.' This approach has left White House staff scrambling to communicate policy achievements in ways that appeal to voters, such as linking Small Business Administration fraud prevention and Environmental Protection Agency regulation rollbacks to affordability. However, these messaging efforts appear largely ineffective, with Trump's approval rating hitting a record low of 38 percent in a New York Times/Siena survey and economic confidence at a four-year low according to Gallup.

item.title: Report alleges Treasury officials pushed proposal to place Donald Trump’s image on U.S. currency despite legal restrictions
item.description:
An investigative report alleges that two political appointees in the U.S. Treasury Department pressured staff at the Bureau of Engraving and Printing to advance a proposal that would place former President Donald Trump’s portrait on U.S. currency, specifically a proposed $250 bill. According to the reporting, U.S. Treasurer Brandon Beach and senior adviser Mike Brown repeatedly pushed bureau officials to proceed with the concept, even after career staff warned that it would violate federal law. U.S. law has prohibited living individuals from appearing on American currency since 1866, making the proposal legally problematic. Bureau officials, including then-director Patricia Solimene, reportedly objected to the plan, emphasizing that currency design is a lengthy process that typically requires six to eight years and extensive interagency coordination.
Despite these objections, the appointees allegedly continued to promote mock-up designs showing Trump’s face on the proposed bill. Solim

item.title: Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito's Son Works as Attorney at Treasury Department During Trump Administration
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito faces potential conflict of interest concerns after reports revealed that his son, Philip Alito, has been working as a political appointee attorney in the U.S. Treasury Department's office of the general counsel since the early months of President Donald Trump's second administration. According to sources who spoke with NOTUS, Philip Alito has maintained a low profile, introducing himself simply as 'Phil' and avoiding drawing attention to his family name. He has no public resume or LinkedIn profile, and his name does not appear on the Treasury website. As an attorney-adviser in the general counsel's front office, he was briefed on significant Treasury matters involving taxation, economic policy, and law enforcement. This role has raised questions due to ongoing high-stakes cases involving the Treasury Department that have reached or could reach the Supreme Court. Notably, while Philip Alito was employed there, a lawsuit challenging Trump's use of emer

item.title: Chicago Teen and Mother Released from ICE Detention Following Legal and Community Pressure
item.description: Ricardo Hernandez-Navarrete, an 18-year-old from Chicago, and his mother, Martha Liliana Navarrete-Capazan, were detained by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) in March. They were separated and moved across multiple detention facilities in seven states. Following legal petitions and widespread community and political advocacy, Martha was released on May 19, and Ricardo was released shortly after, in time to attend his high school graduation. Their lawyer, Kelli Fennell, noted that Ricardo was placed on a wrist monitor instead of an ankle monitor, which allowed him to continue playing soccer. Both mother and son continue to pursue asylum claims due to fears of persecution in Colombia. The case drew attention from lawmakers, including Rep. Jan Schakowsky and Sen. Dick Durbin, who condemned the treatment they endured and criticized the administration's immigration policies. Community organizer Kristy Morrow spearheaded fundraising and logistical support for the family, highlig

item.title: X-Bow certified for full-scale rocket motor production as Pentagon seeks to expand supply chain
item.description:
U.S. defense startup X-Bow Systems has achieved a key milestone by receiving certification for full production operations at its Texas facility, marking a significant step in efforts to strengthen the country’s solid rocket motor supply chain. The certification was obtained in collaboration with the U.S. Air Force Research Laboratory under the RE-ARM (Rapid Energetics & Advanced Rocket Manufacturing) program, which focuses on advancing scalable and efficient missile manufacturing technologies.
This development comes as the Pentagon faces growing pressure to replenish missile stockpiles amid ongoing global conflicts, including those in Ukraine, Israel, and Iran. For years, the U.S. rocket motor market has been dominated by a small number of suppliers, primarily Northrop Grumman and L3Harris. X-Bow’s entry into full-scale production represents an effort to diversify suppliers and improve production resilience.
The company’s Texas facility includes two main systems: a fixed-site product

item.title: Trump refiles defamation lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein-related report
item.description:
President Donald Trump has refiled a defamation lawsuit against The Wall Street Journal after an earlier version of the case was dismissed by a federal judge. The lawsuit centers on a report alleging that Trump once sent Jeffrey Epstein a crude birthday card featuring a sexualized message and an explicit doodle of a woman. Trump has denied that the card exists, although it was later released as part of materials connected to the Epstein investigation.
The original lawsuit was thrown out by U.S. District Judge Darrin Gayles, who found that Trump, as a public figure, failed to adequately allege that the newspaper acted with “actual malice,” a legal requirement under defamation law established by New York Times v. Sullivan. The judge concluded that the complaint did not sufficiently demonstrate that the publication knowingly published false information or recklessly disregarded the truth.
In the revised filing, Trump’s legal team attempts to address this deficiency by arguing that the n

item.title: New Superconductor Reaches 151 K at Ambient Pressure, Setting a Long-Standing Record
item.description:
Researchers at the University of Houston have achieved a significant milestone in superconductivity by developing a material that operates at 151 Kelvin (−122°C) under normal atmospheric pressure. This sets a new record for the highest superconducting transition temperature under ambient conditions, surpassing the previous benchmark of 133 K established in 1993 with a mercury-based cuprate. Superconductors are materials that conduct electricity without resistance, eliminating energy loss as heat and offering major potential benefits for power transmission, medical imaging, quantum technologies, and advanced electronics.
The breakthrough was made by scientists from the Texas Center for Superconductivity and the university’s physics department, led by Ching-Wu Chu and Liangzi Deng. Their approach relied on a technique called pressure quenching. In this process, the material is first subjected to extremely high pressure to enhance its superconducting properties. While still under pressur

item.title: Concerns Over Government-Influenced Stock Gains and Market Distortion
item.description:
This opinion article examines the recent surge in Intel’s stock performance and raises concerns about what the author sees as distortions in capitalist markets when government involvement and successful public investment play a role. The piece uses a metaphor comparing inexperienced gamblers who mistake early luck for skill, suggesting that a similar dynamic may be occurring in financial markets when investors or institutions interpret short-term gains as evidence of sustainable success.
The author argues that Intel’s stock boom, fueled in part by a successful investment linked to government activity, may be creating a misleading impression of strength in the company and in broader market dynamics. According to the commentary, such situations can encourage overconfidence among investors and policymakers, potentially leading to misallocation of capital and increased risk-taking based on temporary or externally supported gains rather than underlying fundamentals.
The article extends th

item.title: Street Fighter 6 Sets Server Maintenance for Ingrid Release and Season 3 Conclusion Update
item.description: Street Fighter 6 is preparing for its biggest content drop since launching in 2023 as Season 3 comes to a close. Capcom has announced server maintenance on May 27, 2026, starting at 7 p.m. PDT and expected to last approximately five hours until 12 a.m. PDT. During this period, all online features including matchmaking, Battle Hub, shop, and leaderboards will be unavailable. Players will be able to download the update shortly after maintenance begins. Those who own the Year 3 Character Pass can immediately try Ingrid in offline modes. Ingrid, a mysterious magical girl character previously seen in other Capcom titles, enters the mainline roster as a technical zoner. Her kit includes multiple projectile options, Sun Crests that stock up to power up attacks, and teleportation abilities. The update also introduces two brand new game modes: Avatar Arcade and Random Avatar Matches. These modes expand avatar customization options and provide fresh ways to play both against CPUs and other playe

item.title: Knife attack at Winterthur train station injures three; suspect arrested
item.description:
A knife attack at a train station in the Swiss city of Winterthur, near Zurich, left three people injured early Thursday morning, according to local police and Reuters reporting. Authorities said the incident occurred shortly after 2:30 a.m. ET, when a man allegedly attacked multiple individuals at the station. Emergency services responded quickly, and the three victims were taken to the hospital for medical treatment. Their current conditions were not immediately disclosed.
Police confirmed that a 31-year-old Swiss national was arrested in connection with the attack. An investigation has been launched to determine the circumstances and possible motive behind the incident. The attack triggered a significant police response and disrupted normal activity at the station.
Swiss newspaper Blick reported that it obtained video footage allegedly showing a man running through the station concourse while shouting “Allahu Akbar,” an Arabic phrase meaning “God is great.” The report also cited a

item.title: Trump Endorsement of Paxton Raises Financial and Electoral Concerns for GOP in Texas Senate Race
item.description: Ken Paxton’s surprise primary win over Senator John Cornyn in Texas has alarmed Republican strategists and donors, who fear the ensuing general election could demand unprecedented financial resources. Analysts and GOP insiders told Politico that defending the Texas seat could require up to $150 million, money that might otherwise support competitive races in states like Maine, Michigan, Ohio, and North Carolina. Cornyn, a seasoned fundraiser, typically relied on his extensive donor network, whereas Paxton entered the runoff with only $2.3 million. Trump's last-minute endorsement of Paxton has intensified concerns, as strategists warn the party may face a 'horror movie' scenario if Paxton struggles to appeal beyond MAGA voters. Democratic challenger James Talarico, by contrast, quickly raised $600,000 in two hours following Paxton's victory, signaling a potentially expensive race. Overall spending in the Texas Senate contest could surpass $550 million, more than double the $210 million

item.title: Carroll Baker’s Role as Jean Harlow in the 1965 Biopic and Its Cinematic Impact
item.description: Carroll Baker's portrayal of Jean Harlow in the 1965 Paramount biopic 'Harlow' showcased her glamour and screen presence, though the film was widely criticized for historical inaccuracies. Originally, Marilyn Monroe was intended for the lead role, but after her death, producer Joseph E. Levine acquired the rights to Irving Shulman's controversial biography for $100,000. Baker, fresh from her Harlow-inspired performance in 'The Carpetbaggers,' became the natural choice after a tense contract dispute with Columbia Pictures, ultimately resolved with a lavish platinum and diamond necklace from Levine. The production faced further challenges, including competition from a low-budget rival film starring Carol Lynley released shortly before Paramount’s version. Despite its melodramatic script, the film received praise for its visual and costume design, particularly from Edith Head, who reinterpreted Harlow's 1930s style to suit 1965 cinematic aesthetics. Baker’s performance captured both vulne

item.title: Ken Paxton Defeats Senator John Cornyn in Texas Republican Primary Runoff
item.description:
Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton defeated longtime U.S. Senator John Cornyn in a closely watched Republican primary runoff, marking a significant shift within the state’s GOP and highlighting the continued influence of former President Donald Trump. Cornyn, one of the most senior Republican figures in the Senate, lost to Paxton after the latter secured a late endorsement from Trump, which helped energize conservative voters.
The race drew national attention as a test of establishment Republicans versus candidates aligned with Trump’s political movement. Paxton framed his campaign as a challenge to party elites, appealing to grassroots conservatives despite ongoing controversies surrounding his tenure, including legal investigations and a prior impeachment battle. With approximately 76% of votes counted on election night, Paxton led Cornyn by a wide margin of 28 percentage points, and the Associated Press called the race shortly after polls closed.
Cornyn accepted the results and ple

item.title: Vertu launches $6,880 AI-powered foldable smartphone targeting enterprise executives
item.description:
Luxury smartphone maker Vertu has unveiled a new foldable device designed specifically for business executives who manage operations on the move. The device, called Alphafold, starts at $6,880 for its base calfskin version, while premium editions featuring materials such as alligator leather, 18K gold, and diamond accents can reach up to $46,800, with additional customizations available. Vertu is positioning the product as a high-end status device that merges luxury hardware with enterprise-grade artificial intelligence capabilities.
At the core of Alphafold is an AI agent system called Hermes Agent, built on an open-source framework, which is designed to connect with enterprise systems such as ERP and CRM platforms. The agent can handle tasks including approvals, scheduling, sales tracking, travel coordination, and operational reporting through natural-language commands. The system is also designed to route requests across multiple AI models, including offerings from OpenAI, Anthropi

item.title: Retired federal judges challenge legality of Trump-related DOJ settlement and $1.8 billion fund
item.description:
A bipartisan group of 35 retired federal judges has filed a motion in a Florida federal court challenging a controversial settlement involving Donald Trump, his family, and the Department of Justice. According to reporting cited from The Washington Post, the judges argue that the agreement—linked to a $1.8 billion fund described by critics as a politically motivated “weaponization” or compensation pool—may have been improperly concealed from the court and could constitute fraud or judicial manipulation.
The underlying case stems from a lawsuit originally filed by Trump, his sons, and the Trump Organization against the IRS in January, seeking up to $10 billion in damages over allegations that a former agency consultant leaked their tax information to the press. The judges’ filing claims that Trump and federal prosecutors failed to disclose a subsequent settlement when requesting dismissal of the case, raising concerns about transparency and judicial integrity.
The retired judges furth

item.title: AI Phone Monitoring Shows Promise in Reducing Hospitalizations for Heart Failure Patients
item.description:
A study led by cardiologist Aleix Olivella at Vall d’Hebron University Hospital explores the use of conversational artificial intelligence to remotely monitor patients with heart failure, a chronic and often underrecognized condition that is a leading cause of hospitalization among older adults. Rather than relying on complex apps or devices, the system uses a simple and familiar tool: weekly automated phone calls.
The AI system contacts patients and asks about common warning signs of worsening heart failure, such as shortness of breath, fatigue, or sudden weight gain due to fluid retention. Importantly, the AI does not make clinical decisions. Instead, it collects and organizes patient responses and flags potential concerns for review by healthcare professionals, who then determine whether intervention is needed.
The study included thousands of automated calls, with over 90% successfully completed and high patient satisfaction reported. Many participants expressed reassurance from t

item.title: Judge allows Florida teen accused in cruise ship killing to remain with family pending detention decision
item.description:
A U.S. federal judge has decided not to immediately detain a 16-year-old Florida boy accused of sexually assaulting and killing his 18-year-old stepsister, Anna Kepner, aboard a Carnival cruise ship in November 2025. The teenager, Timothy Hudson, has pleaded not guilty to charges of murder and aggravated sexual abuse and will remain in the custody of a family member under supervision while the court considers where he should be held.
Hudson was initially arrested as a juvenile shortly after Kepner’s death and allowed to live with an uncle under electronic monitoring. In February, a federal grand jury indicted him as an adult, increasing the likelihood of pretrial detention. The case is being handled in federal court because the alleged crime occurred in international waters, outside state jurisdiction.
During a hearing in Miami, U.S. District Judge Edwin Torres noted that while an adult defendant under similar circumstances would likely be detained, Hudson’s age warranted additional

item.title: Late-April Freeze Causes Major Crop Losses Across Northeast Orchards
item.description:
A rare late-April freeze has caused severe agricultural damage across the Northeastern United States, particularly in New Jersey and surrounding states, wiping out significant portions of apple and peach crops. Farmers describe the event as unprecedented, with some calling it a “generational freeze” that struck at a critical stage of fruit development, leaving orchards with little to no viable harvest for the season. In New Jersey alone, state agriculture officials estimate losses of approximately $300 million, affecting hundreds of farms and threatening the economic stability of local agricultural communities.
At Terhune Orchards in Princeton, farmers report catastrophic damage, noting that multiple fruit crops were lost simultaneously, an unusual and highly damaging occurrence. The freeze also impacted Pennsylvania, another major peach-producing state, raising concerns about regional shortages of fresh fruit across the Northeast and potential ripple effects in supply chains.
State

item.title: Critics Condemn Proposed January 6 Compensation Fund as Officers Sue to Block It
item.description:
The article is an opinion piece criticizing a proposed federal compensation fund connected to individuals prosecuted for their involvement in the January 6, 2021 Capitol attack. The author argues that the fund, reportedly backed by acting Attorney General Todd Blanche and associated with former President Donald Trump, represents a misuse of taxpayer money and an attempt to rewrite public understanding of the Capitol riot. According to the piece, Metropolitan Police officer Daniel Hodges and former Capitol Police officer Harry Dunn have filed a lawsuit seeking to block the creation of the fund, describing it as an unprecedented act of political corruption.
The article revisits the events of January 6 in detail, emphasizing the violence directed at law enforcement officers defending the Capitol while Congress certified the 2020 presidential election results. It recounts how rioters breached police barricades, assaulted officers with weapons and chemicals, damaged government property, an

item.title: Matthew Perry's Personal Assistant Sentenced to 41 Months in Prison for Role in Ketamine Overdose Death
item.description: Kenneth Iwamasa, the live-in personal assistant to the late 'Friends' actor Matthew Perry, was sentenced to three years and five months (41 months) in federal prison for his central role in the ketamine overdose that caused Perry's death on October 28, 2023. Iwamasa pleaded guilty to one count of conspiracy to distribute ketamine resulting in death. Prosecutors described him as Perry's enabler who sourced the drug, administered multiple injections daily without medical training, and was the last person to see the actor alive before finding him unresponsive in his hot tub. The judge also imposed two years of supervised release and a $10,000 fine. Iwamasa was the first of five defendants to reach a plea deal and the final one sentenced in the case. He expressed regret in court, stating he would 'forever regret' his actions. Defense lawyers argued Iwamasa was following his employer's demands and had difficulty saying no due to the nature of their relationship. Perry's mother addressed the

item.title: Opinion piece argues that U.S. democracy is heavily influenced by money and lobbying power
item.description:
The article is an opinion commentary that questions the integrity of democratic processes in the United States, arguing that political outcomes are heavily shaped by wealthy donors, lobbying groups, and corporate influence rather than the will of ordinary voters. It highlights recent U.S. congressional primary elections in which large sums of money were reportedly spent by political action committees and interest groups to support or oppose specific candidates. According to the piece, these financial interventions are presented as evidence that elections can be significantly swayed by concentrated wealth, particularly through organizations associated with foreign policy interests.
The author uses examples of high-cost primary races to argue that candidates who challenge dominant policy positions or powerful interest groups are often outspent and defeated. The article extends this argument to broader criticisms of U.S. foreign policy, suggesting a disconnect between democratic rhetoric

item.title: Ron Baron Bets Heavily on Elon Musk’s Companies as SpaceX Nears a Possible IPO
item.description:
The article focuses on Ron Baron, the 83-year-old celebrity investor whose firm has made a major bet on Elon Musk’s companies, especially SpaceX and Tesla. Baron’s largest fund has had a weak year, but his long-term optimism is tied to the value of Musk’s private and public ventures. He first invested in SpaceX in 2017, when the company was valued at less than $22 billion, and that position has grown into a very large share of his portfolio.
As of last month, SpaceX made up nearly 30% of Baron’s largest fund, while Tesla represented almost 19%, showing how concentrated his exposure to Musk has become. The article says Baron’s funds collectively hold more than $6 billion in SpaceX shares. That concentration reflects Baron’s confidence that Musk’s companies could deliver outsized gains, especially if SpaceX’s long-anticipated stock-market debut proceeds and the company’s valuation rises sharply.
The piece frames Baron’s strategy as both bold and risky. His funds have lagged this year a

item.title: First Afghan Woman Successfully Climbs Mount Everest
item.description:
An Afghan woman has achieved a historic milestone by becoming the first woman from Afghanistan to successfully climb Mount Everest, the world’s highest peak. The achievement was reported by CNN, with correspondent Isa Soares covering the story and highlighting the significance of the accomplishment for both the climber and her country. The mountaineer, identified as River Ahmad, completed the ascent of Mount Everest, which stands at 8,849 meters (29,032 feet) above sea level and is widely considered one of the most challenging feats in extreme sports and mountaineering.
Her successful summit represents a groundbreaking moment for Afghan women in athletics and adventure sports, a field where participation has historically been limited due to cultural, social, and political constraints. The climb not only demonstrates extraordinary physical endurance and technical skill but also symbolizes resilience and determination in the face of barriers. According to the report, Ahmad’s achievement

item.title: New Jersey proposes rules requiring data centers to cover their own energy and infrastructure costs
item.description:
New Jersey Governor Mikie Sherrill has unveiled a new policy proposal aimed at changing how data centers operate in the state, particularly in relation to their energy consumption and impact on public utilities. The plan requires data centers to directly pay for the electricity they use and to take financial responsibility for upgrades needed to support their heavy demand on the power grid. According to the proposal, legislation is being developed that would also require transparency from data centers regarding their energy and water usage, with mandatory public reporting every six months. The governor emphasized that large facilities, some of which consume as much as 300 megawatts of electricity, should no longer rely on ratepayer subsidies and instead fund their own infrastructure needs, including grid expansion and maintenance.
The initiative also includes provisions for community development agreements, ensuring that data centers contribute financially to the local communities wh

item.title: Gray Malin’s ‘Art of Living’ Series Places Mid-Century Furniture on Floating Platforms in Bora Bora
item.description: The article showcases a visual art project by photographer Gray Malin titled “Art of Living,” which continues his exploration of playful, stylized environments. In this series, Malin stages mid-century modern furniture on a mirrored floating platform set against the tropical backdrop of French Polynesia, specifically Bora Bora. The combination of sleek vintage design and surreal placement in an aquatic environment creates a dreamlike and slightly eccentric visual experience. The mirrored surfaces amplify the scenery, blending sky, water, and furniture into a cohesive and almost illusionary composition. The result is described as both whimsical and visually striking, inviting viewers into an imagined leisure space that feels both familiar and otherworldly. The project reflects Malin’s signature approach to photography, which emphasizes color, composition, and the transformation of everyday objects into conceptual art installations. According to the article, the photographs are available

item.title: Diane Cilento, Australian Actress Known for Film, Theatre, and Independent Spirit
item.description:
Diane Cilento (1933–2011) was an Australian actress celebrated for her beauty, intelligence, and commanding screen presence. She rose to prominence during the 1950s and 1960s, a period in which she established herself as a respected performer in both film and theater. Cilento earned critical recognition for her versatility and ability to bring depth to complex characters, standing out in an era when many actresses were often confined to stereotypical roles.
Among her most notable screen appearances were films such as 'The Naked Edge' (1961) and 'Hombre' (1967), where she demonstrated strong dramatic range and presence. She is also remembered for her role as Willow in the cult horror classic 'The Wicker Man' (1973), a performance that contributed to the film’s enduring legacy in cinematic history. Her career reflected both mainstream success and participation in more unconventional projects, highlighting her willingness to explore diverse artistic paths.
Beyond her professional life,

item.title: Reader advice requested: difficulty pronouncing a family member’s partner’s name
item.description:
The article presents a weekly reader participation feature from The Washington Post’s advice column series centered on Carolyn Hax. In this installment, readers are invited to respond to a submitted question rather than receiving a direct answer from the columnist. The prompt focuses on a relatable social dilemma: a person is struggling to pronounce their brother’s boyfriend’s name and is seeking advice on how to handle the situation respectfully and comfortably. The format is part of a recurring editorial approach in which the columnist is unable to address every submitted question, so selected reader responses are featured instead.
The piece emphasizes community engagement, encouraging readers to think critically and empathetically about interpersonal relationships and everyday communication challenges. Although the specific question is simple, it touches on broader themes such as inclusion, respect for identity, cultural sensitivity in pronouncing names correctly, and navigating aw

item.title: Dow closes at record high as falling oil prices and easing geopolitical tensions support markets
item.description:
U.S. stocks closed mostly higher on May 27, 2026, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average reaching a new record as declining oil prices and optimism around easing tensions with Iran supported investor sentiment. The Dow gained 182.60 points, or 0.36%, finishing at 50,644.28, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite posted marginal gains, both also ending at record levels. Oil prices dropped sharply, with U.S. crude falling more than 5% to settle below $89 per barrel after reports suggested Iran could restore commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, although the White House disputed those claims.
Technology stocks showed mixed performance, particularly in the semiconductor sector. While Micron Technology extended its rally—driven by strong demand linked to artificial intelligence—other chipmakers such as Intel and Qualcomm declined. Analysts cautioned that valuations in the semiconductor space may be overheated despite long-term growth prospects tied to AI.
Financial stocks were

item.title: U.S. Launches Additional Defensive Strikes on Iran Amid Ongoing Ceasefire Negotiations
item.description:
The United States carried out additional military strikes against Iranian targets after U.S. forces intercepted several Iranian attack drones near the Strait of Hormuz. According to U.S. officials, American forces shot down four Iranian one-way drones considered a threat to regional security and also struck a ground control station in Bandar Abbas that was reportedly preparing to launch another drone. The actions were described by the Pentagon as defensive operations during a fragile ceasefire period.
President Donald Trump stated during a Cabinet meeting that Iran was weakening economically and politically, saying the country was “negotiating on fumes.” He expressed confidence that a broader agreement could eventually be reached to reduce tensions and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, an important global oil shipping route. Trump also insisted that upcoming U.S. midterm elections would not influence his negotiating strategy.
The emerging negotiations reportedly include Iran giving up its

item.title: Jill Biden Says Joe Biden’s 2024 Debate Performance Raised Fears of a Medical Emergency
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden said in a CBS News interview that she was deeply alarmed by former President Joe Biden’s performance during the June 2024 presidential debate against Donald Trump. According to Jill Biden, watching her husband struggle through the debate made her fear he was experiencing a serious medical issue, including the possibility of a stroke. She explained that she had never seen him behave that way before and that the moment 'scared me to death.' The debate became a major turning point in the 2024 presidential race, as Biden’s unclear answers, low energy, and frail appearance intensified concerns among Democrats and voters about his age and ability to continue serving as president. Despite public support from White House officials and campaign allies immediately after the debate, pressure quickly mounted for Biden to withdraw from the race. Jill Biden herself publicly defended her husband in the days following the event, praising his performance at a campaign rally

item.title: The Narrow Door at Portugal’s Alcobaça Monastery and the Legend of the “Fat-Catcher” Door
item.description:
The article explores the famous narrow doorway at the Alcobaça Monastery (Mosteiro de Santa Maria de Alcobaça) in Portugal, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the most significant medieval Cistercian monasteries in Europe. Founded in 1153 by King Afonso Henriques and entrusted to the Cistercian Order, the monastery is known for its austere Gothic architecture, reflecting monastic values of simplicity, discipline, and labor.
One of its most talked-about architectural features is a small passage between the kitchen and the refectory (dining hall), commonly referred to in popular tradition as the “Porta Pega-Gordos” or “Fat-Catcher Door.” According to a widespread modern legend, this narrow doorway—often claimed to be extremely tight—was designed to force monks to remain thin. The story suggests that monks who became too overweight due to gluttony would be unable to pass through and would be required to fast until they regained their discipline, reinforcing monastic ideals of temper

item.title: WHO Warns Conflict in Eastern DRC Is Hindering Efforts to Contain Ebola Outbreak
item.description:
The World Health Organization (WHO) has warned that the ongoing armed conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) is severely undermining efforts to contain a growing Ebola outbreak in Ituri province. WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus described the situation as a “catastrophic collision” between disease and conflict, emphasizing that violence in the region is preventing medical teams from reaching affected communities and tracking infections effectively.
According to the WHO, at least 10 confirmed Ebola deaths and around 220 suspected deaths have been recorded since mid-May 2026. Nearly 900 suspected cases have also been identified since the DRC officially declared the outbreak on May 15. Health officials believe the actual number of infections could be significantly higher due to limited access to remote and conflict-affected areas.
The Ebola strain involved in the outbreak is the Bundibugyo variant, which currently has no approved vaccine or specific treatm

item.title: Legal expert raises concerns over Trump judicial nominees’ refusal to affirm 2020 election results
item.description:
A legal expert has expressed alarm over what he describes as a troubling pattern among several judicial nominees put forward by President Donald Trump. During recent Senate Judiciary Committee hearings, some nominees were directly asked who won the 2020 U.S. presidential election. Despite repeated questioning from Democratic senators, multiple nominees declined to clearly state that Joe Biden won the election, instead offering evasive or non-committal responses.
Andrew Weissmann, a former federal prosecutor, criticized this behavior during an appearance on a podcast, arguing that the refusal to acknowledge the widely accepted outcome of the 2020 election is deeply concerning. He stated that there is no credible evidence of widespread fraud that would have changed the result and noted that claims of a rigged election have been repeatedly rejected in court. Weissmann suggested that acknowledging the lack of evidence for fraud should be a basic and legitimate response for judicial nomine

item.title: Former Army General Criticizes Pete Hegseth's West Point Remarks on Military Accountability
item.description:
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced criticism following a commencement speech delivered at the U.S. Military Academy at West Point on May 23, 2026. During the address, Hegseth spoke about the Trump administration’s support for military personnel and reiterated several culture-war themes, including opposition to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion initiatives within the armed forces. However, the most controversial part of the speech involved his comments suggesting that military personnel would receive unconditional backing from the administration, even when battlefield decisions turned out to be wrong.
Hegseth told graduates that President Donald Trump and his administration would support service members regardless of whether decisions made in combat were right or wrong, and regardless of the motivations behind those actions. His remarks quickly drew criticism from retired military officials and commentators who viewed the statement as undermining military discipline and legal accounta

item.title: Tencent launches inbound payment upgrade to simplify payments for international visitors ahead of APEC 2026
item.description:
Tencent has announced a major upgrade to its inbound payment ecosystem as part of the “2026 Inbound Payment Service Upgrade Initiative,” unveiled at the Shenzhen International Financial Expo in preparation for APEC 2026. The initiative aims to make digital payments significantly easier for international travelers visiting China by enhancing product capabilities, expanding global wallet partnerships, and improving multilingual service coverage.
A key highlight is the collaboration between TenPay Global and PayPal World, which enables PayPal users to pay at millions of Weixin Pay merchants in China by scanning QR codes. The service will first launch for users in the United States, with additional markets to follow. This integration allows international visitors to use their familiar digital wallets without needing to download new apps, reinforcing Tencent’s “Pay with Your Home Wallet” model already supported by more than 40 overseas wallet institutions.
Tencent also introduced a 90-day

item.title: Lindsey Graham Draws Criticism After Suggesting Trump Deserves a Nobel Prize for Middle East Diplomacy
item.description:
Sen. Lindsey Graham appeared on Fox News with host Sean Hannity to discuss President Donald Trump’s foreign policy efforts in the Middle East, including ongoing negotiations related to the conflict involving Iran and Israel. During the interview, Graham made a controversial statement suggesting that if Trump were able to convince Saudi Arabia to formally recognize Israel, it could effectively end the long-running Israeli-Arab conflict. Graham went further by saying that the Nobel Prize should be renamed the “Trump Prize” if such a diplomatic breakthrough occurred.
The comments quickly generated criticism online from political analysts, commentators, and social media users. Critics argued that Graham’s remarks oversimplified the history and complexity of Middle Eastern conflicts and exaggerated the potential impact of a single diplomatic agreement. Some commentators specifically challenged Graham’s characterization of Saudi Arabia as “the epicenter of Islam” and objected to his suggest

item.title: Norm Eisen Criticizes Trump Administration Over Criminal Investigation Into E. Jean Carroll
item.description:
A Raw Story article reports on criticism directed at the Trump administration after news emerged that the Department of Justice had launched a criminal investigation into writer E. Jean Carroll. Carroll previously accused President Donald Trump of raping her in a Manhattan department store during the 1990s. In earlier civil proceedings, a jury found Trump liable for sexual abuse and defamation, awarding Carroll damages. Trump has consistently denied the allegations and has continued to challenge the verdict through multiple appeals.
During a CNN interview with Anderson Cooper, former White House ethics chief Norm Eisen strongly condemned the reported DOJ action. Eisen described the investigation as an abuse of presidential authority and argued that Trump was using federal law enforcement powers against perceived political adversaries. He compared the situation to previous cases involving figures such as New York Attorney General Letitia James and former FBI Director James Comey, claim

item.title: Texas Senate Race Between Ken Paxton and James Talarico Raises Financial Concerns for Republicans
item.description:
The upcoming Texas Senate race between Republican Attorney General Ken Paxton and Democratic state Representative James Talarico is already creating concern among Republican strategists because of the enormous financial demands expected during the campaign. According to the report, Republicans spent more than $100 million during the primary contest, much of it unsuccessfully supporting longtime Senator John Cornyn against Paxton, who ultimately secured the Republican nomination with former President Donald Trump’s endorsement.
Political observers believe the general election could become one of the most expensive Senate races in the country. Republicans are especially worried because Paxton has significantly weaker fundraising numbers compared to Talarico. As of early May, Paxton had raised approximately $7.6 million and retained only about $2.3 million in campaign funds. Meanwhile, Talarico has already raised more than $40 million during his campaign efforts, and his fundraising acce

item.title: Gretchen Carlson Criticizes DOJ Investigation Into E. Jean Carroll Case and Related Actions
item.description:
Former Fox News anchor Gretchen Carlson expressed strong criticism during a CNN appearance regarding recent actions by the U.S. Department of Justice under President Donald Trump. Carlson reacted sharply to a new criminal investigation involving E. Jean Carroll, who has previously accused Trump of sexual abuse and has won defamation lawsuits against him. She questioned the DOJ’s priorities, suggesting that its focus on certain cases appears politically motivated rather than centered on public interest.
During the interview, Carlson referenced her own past legal battle against Fox News over sexual harassment allegations, stating that the issue is personally significant to her. She emphasized concern over what she described as the government targeting individuals who have spoken out about misconduct. Carlson argued that the DOJ is spending resources on cases she believes are not credible, framing the situation as part of a broader pattern of political retaliation.
She further claimed t

item.title: Commentator raises concerns after video shows Trump swaying during Memorial Day ceremony
item.description:
A political commentator sparked online discussion after analyzing a video from a Memorial Day ceremony in which U.S. President Donald Trump appeared to sway while standing during a military salute at Arlington National Cemetery. The footage, recorded during a public ceremony earlier in the week, shows Trump standing alongside Vice President JD Vance and other officials. According to commentator David Pakman, Trump appeared to shift his weight repeatedly and struggle to maintain balance, while those standing nearby remained still. Pakman highlighted the contrast in movement, suggesting that the president’s posture and stability appeared unusual compared to the others present.
The commentary video further referenced additional clips in which Trump is seen walking in a straight line on a red carpet and moving away from a helicopter, both of which were presented as examples of potentially uneven or unsteady movement. Pakman described the behavior as concerning and suggested that it could

item.title: Spencer Pratt distinguishes his Los Angeles mayoral campaign from Trump on immigration policy
item.description:
Los Angeles mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt, a former reality TV personality, has drawn comparisons to President Donald Trump due to their shared background in entertainment and similar emphasis on tough-on-crime messaging. However, Pratt has made clear distinctions between his political views and Trump’s, particularly regarding immigration enforcement. While he supports the removal of individuals convicted of serious violent crimes, he explicitly opposes aggressive ICE raids targeting places such as restaurants and retail stores. Pratt stated that he does not want Immigration and Customs Enforcement conducting raids in everyday workplaces, signaling a more limited approach to enforcement compared to Trump’s broader policies.
Pratt’s campaign platform also includes a major proposal to build a large-scale treatment facility on federal land aimed at housing thousands of homeless individuals struggling with substance use disorders. The ambitious infrastructure plan has drawn both attenti

item.title: Federal Judge Orders Customs Chief to Explain Delays in Trump Tariff Refunds
item.description:
A federal trade court judge has ordered Rodney S. Scott, the head of U.S. Customs and Border Protection under President Donald Trump, to appear at a hearing concerning the administration’s handling of refunds connected to tariffs previously ruled illegal by the Supreme Court. The dispute centers on Trump’s earlier “reciprocal tariffs,” which had been implemented using the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), despite criticism that the law does not explicitly authorize tariffs.
After the Supreme Court invalidated the tariff program earlier in 2026, the Court of International Trade directed the government to begin refunding money collected from importers. Although the administration publicly resisted returning the funds, it eventually established a refund process and began accepting repayment requests in late April.
However, concerns emerged after government filings indicated that Customs could process refunds for only about $127 billion of the estimated $166 billion co

item.title: Bill Maher critiques Trump’s handling of Iran conflict while supporting overall strategy
item.description:
Comedian Bill Maher commented on the Trump administration’s handling of the ongoing conflict involving Iran during an appearance on the podcast “Hot Mics With Billy Bush.” While Maher stated that he generally supports the U.S.-Israel military actions regarding Iran and described them as a “good attempt,” he also criticized the timing and execution of the strategy, arguing that the administration “missed their window” for potential regime change.
Maher suggested that earlier intervention might have been more effective, particularly during periods of widespread anti-government protests in Iran. He argued that the opportunity for revolution diminished after the Iranian government’s crackdown on demonstrators earlier in the year, which reportedly resulted in significant casualties. According to Maher, the delay in action allowed the situation to deteriorate and reduced the likelihood of successful internal opposition leadership emerging.
Despite his criticisms, Maher did not reject the p

item.title: Vintage Photos Showcase Mid-Century Curtain Design Trends of the 1950s and 1960s
item.description:
The article explores the popularity and stylistic importance of curtains during the 1950s and 1960s, a period described as the “Golden Age of Curtains.” According to the feature, window treatments became a major part of home decoration during the mid-century era, moving beyond their practical role to serve as fashionable design statements in American and European homes. The article highlights how homeowners and interior designers embraced luxurious fabrics, decorative textures, and elaborate drapery styles that reflected the optimism and prosperity of the postwar years.
The collection of 35 vintage photographs showcases a wide variety of curtain styles that were common at the time. These include floor-length drapes, ornate valances, sheer café curtains, and carefully tailored pinch-pleat designs. Many of the curtains featured bold and colorful patterns inspired by mid-century aesthetics, including atomic motifs, floral prints, and geometric designs. The article emphasizes the craftsma

item.title: Trump sparks controversy after warning Oman over Strait of Hormuz remarks during Cabinet meeting
item.description: During a Cabinet meeting held at the White House, U.S. President Donald Trump made controversial remarks regarding the strategic Strait of Hormuz and the role of regional countries, including Oman. While responding to questions about a potential agreement to end ongoing tensions involving Iran, Trump rejected the idea of transferring control or influence over the waterway to Iran or Oman. He emphasized that the Strait is an international passage that should remain open to all countries. In his comments, Trump stated that Oman would need to “behave like everybody else,” adding that if it did not, the United States would “have to blow them up,” a remark that immediately drew widespread attention and criticism. The statement prompted a strong reaction across political and media circles, with commentators expressing shock, sarcasm, and concern about the implications of such rhetoric toward a U.S. ally. Some observers highlighted the visible reaction of senior officials present at the meeti

item.title: Glenn Kirschner Questions Pam Bondi’s Return to Trump Administration and Links It to Epstein File Controversy
item.description:
A new report from Raw Story highlights comments made by former federal prosecutor and 'Justice Matters' podcast host Glenn Kirschner regarding Pam Bondi’s return to President Donald Trump’s administration. According to the article, Kirschner speculated that Bondi’s reappointment could be connected to ongoing efforts to manage or contain information related to the Jeffrey Epstein investigation and associated files.
The article notes that Bondi previously faced criticism from both Republican and Democratic lawmakers over her handling of Epstein-related records while serving as attorney general. During congressional hearings, lawmakers reportedly challenged her responses and oversight concerning the release and management of those files.
Kirschner argued in a reaction video that Bondi may possess sensitive knowledge about individuals potentially connected to Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell. He referenced investigations tied to Epstein’s Zorro Ranch property in New Mexico and suggested tha

item.title: Trump DOJ Removes Online Records Related to January 6 Convictions and Pardoned Rioter
item.description:
CNN anchor Jake Tapper criticized the U.S. Department of Justice after the Trump administration removed online DOJ records connected to individuals convicted for their roles in the January 6 Capitol attack. During a CNN segment, Tapper focused on the case of Andrew Taake, a Texas man who pleaded guilty to assaulting police officers using bear spray and a metal whip during the Capitol riot. Taake had been sentenced to more than six years in prison before later receiving a presidential pardon from Donald Trump.
According to the report, one of the deleted DOJ documents was a 2024 press release that also referenced separate child solicitation charges involving Taake, for which he was later arrested. CNN chief legal affairs correspondent Paula Reid explained that the DOJ justified removing the records by describing them as “partisan propaganda” and claimed the department intended to help individuals it believed were politically persecuted.
Tapper expressed disbelief at the department’s re

item.title: Report Claims Trump Did Not Meet Injured Iran War Soldiers During Walter Reed Visit
item.description:
A report by CBS investigative journalist Michael Kaplan alleges that President Donald Trump did not meet with 14 U.S. soldiers injured during the conflict in Iran while visiting Walter Reed National Military Medical Center earlier this week. According to Kaplan, the information came from both a military official and the family of one of the wounded soldiers. The report states that although Trump was present at the hospital for what the White House described as a six-month medical checkup, officials repeatedly declined to confirm whether he personally visited service members injured during the Iran military campaign.
The article notes that Trump did spend time with other U.S. service members during the visit, but the absence of meetings with soldiers wounded in the conflict has generated criticism and renewed debate over the administration’s handling of military personnel and veterans. The report frames the issue within the broader controversy surrounding the Iran conflict, which the a

item.title: Former Border Patrol commander criticizes proposal to remove CBP from major airports over sanctuary city disputes
item.description:
A former Border Patrol commander under the Trump administration, Gregory Bovino, has publicly criticized a reported proposal by Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin that would remove Customs and Border Protection (CBP) officers from major international airports in liberal cities. The plan is allegedly intended to pressure so-called sanctuary cities by disrupting their ability to process international flights if they do not comply with federal immigration enforcement priorities.
According to the article, the proposed strategy would target major hubs such as JFK in New York and LAX in Los Angeles. However, Bovino opposed the idea not out of disagreement with stricter immigration enforcement, but because he believes it would be strategically counterproductive. In a post on X, he argued that pulling CBP personnel from key airports would amount to a retreat rather than a show of force, effectively conceding political ground to opponents of the policy.
Bovino further suggested that

item.title: Expert disputes Marco Rubio’s claim about U.S. deportation agreements with “safe countries”
item.description:
During a recent Trump administration cabinet meeting, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio claimed that the United States has secured deportation agreements with approximately 20 countries around the world. According to Rubio, these agreements would allow the U.S. to deport individuals to those nations, which he described as “safe” destinations for returned migrants. His comments were presented as part of broader immigration enforcement efforts aimed at expanding international cooperation on deportations.
However, Rubio’s characterization quickly drew sharp criticism from immigration experts. Aaron Reichlin-Melnick, a senior fellow at the American Immigration Council, publicly challenged the claim on social media, arguing that labeling all of these destination countries as “safe” was misleading. He specifically highlighted concerns regarding the Democratic Republic of the Congo, one of the countries reportedly involved in such arrangements.
Reports cited in the discussion suggest that

item.title: Former Federal Judges Seek to Reopen Trump v. IRS Case to Challenge $1.776 Billion Anti-Weaponization Fund
